{{Short description="Process transforming one or more atomic nuclei into one or more different nuclei}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Nuclear reaction
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Nuclear physics]], [[Atomic nucleus]], [[Nuclear fission]], [[Nuclear fusion]], [[Mass–energy equivalence]]
}}

A '''nuclear reaction''' is a process in which one or more [[atomic nucleus|atomic nuclei]] are transformed into one or more different nuclei, often accompanied by the emission or absorption of [[subatomic particle]]s or [[electromagnetic radiation]]. Nuclear reactions are fundamentally different from [[chemical reaction]]s: they involve changes in the [[atomic nucleus|nucleus]] rather than only the [[electron]] cloud, and release energies typically millions of times greater.

== Types of nuclear reactions ==

=== Fission ===
{{Main|Nuclear fission}}
Heavy nucleus splits into lighter nuclei:
:<math>^{235}_{92}\text{U} + n \rightarrow ^{141}_{56}\text{Ba} + ^{92}_{36}\text{Kr} + 3n + \sim200\ \text{MeV}</math>

=== Fusion ===
{{Main|Nuclear fusion}}
Light nuclei combine into heavier nuclei:
:<math>^2\text{D} + ^3\text{T} \rightarrow ^4\text{He} + n + 17.6\ \text{MeV}</math>

=== Radioactive decay ===
{{Main|Radioactive decay}}
Spontaneous transformation:
* [[Alpha decay]]: emission of [[helium-4]] nucleus
* [[Beta decay]]: conversion of neutron ↔ proton + [[electron]]/[[positron]] + [[neutrino]]
* [[Gamma decay]]: emission of high-energy [[photon]]

=== Nuclear scattering ===
* Elastic scattering — kinetic energy conserved
* Inelastic scattering — target nucleus excited

=== Spallation ===
High-energy particle bombardment fragments the target nucleus into multiple lighter nuclei.

== Energy in nuclear reactions ==
The [[Q-value (nuclear)|Q-value]] of a reaction is the energy released (or absorbed):

:<math>Q = (m_{\text{initial}} - m_{\text{final}})c^2</math>

* <math>Q > 0</math> — exothermic (energy released)
* <math>Q < 0</math> — endothermic (energy absorbed)

== Conservation laws ==
Nuclear reactions conserve:
* [[Baryon number]] (total number of nucleons)
* [[Electric charge]]
* [[Energy]] and [[momentum]]
* [[Angular momentum]]
* [[Lepton number]] (in beta decay)

== Applications ==
* [[Nuclear power]] — electricity generation
* [[Nuclear medicine]] — diagnosis and treatment
* [[Radiocarbon dating]] — archaeological dating
* [[Particle accelerator]]s — fundamental research
* [[Nuclear astrophysics]] — understanding stellar processes

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Atomic nucleus]]
* [[Mass–energy equivalence]]
* [[Nuclear fission]]
* [[Nuclear fusion]]
* [[Nuclear physics]]
* [[Radioactive decay]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Nuclear reactions| ]]
[[Category:Nuclear physics]]
